description: Static pool of 20 offline real-world challenges for digital fasting interventions

challenges.title: Take a 10-minute walk outside
challenges.description: Step outside and walk for at least 10 minutes. No phone, no music, no podcasts. Notice three things you haven't seen before in your neighborhood.

challenges.title: Do 20 pushups and stretch
challenges.description: Complete 20 pushups (modified on knees if needed), then hold three stretches for 30 seconds each: hamstrings, shoulders, and neck.

challenges.title: Deep breathing session
challenges.description: Find a quiet spot. Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 4, out for 6. Repeat for 5 minutes. Focus only on the sensation of air moving in and out.

challenges.title: Stair climb challenge
challenges.description: Find a set of stairs and climb up and down 5 times. Take a 1-minute rest at the top between rounds. Count your steps.

challenges.title: Shadow boxing or dance
challenges.description: Put on a song you love (no screen needed - just audio or no music) and move your body for the full duration. Dance, shadow box, or freestyle stretch.

challenges.title: Write a haiku about your day
challenges.description: Write three haikus (5-7-5 syllable structure) about your current state of mind. Use pen and paper only - no digital tools.

challenges.title: Doodle for 10 minutes
challenges.description: Take a blank sheet of paper and doodle freely for 10 minutes. Don't judge the result - the goal is flow, not art. Use at least two colors if available.

challenges.title: Rearrange your workspace
challenges.description: Move three items on your desk or in your room to new positions. Clean one surface completely. Notice how the change affects your focus.

challenges.title: Build a small origami figure
challenges.description: Find a piece of paper and fold it into a simple origami shape (crane, boat, or hat). If you don't know how, invent your own fold pattern.

challenges.title: Write a letter to your future self
challenges.description: Grab a pen and paper. Write a short letter to yourself one year from now. What do you hope has changed? What matters most right now?

challenges.title: Call a friend or family member
challenges.description: Call someone you care about - voice only, no video. Ask them about their day and really listen. Don't mention technology or social media in the conversation.

challenges.title: Give a genuine compliment
challenges.description: Find a colleague, neighbor, or stranger and give them a specific, genuine compliment about something they did or who they are. Notice their reaction.

challenges.title: Write a thank-you note
challenges.description: Write a physical thank-you note to someone who helped you recently. Be specific about what they did and how it affected you. Deliver it if possible.

challenges.title: Have a 5-minute eye-contact conversation
challenges.description: Find someone willing and have a 5-minute conversation while maintaining comfortable eye contact. No phones in sight for either person.

challenges.title: Help someone with a small task
challenges.description: Find someone nearby and offer to help them with something small right now - carrying something, holding a door, or solving a quick problem.

challenges.title: Three good things journal
challenges.description: Write down three things that went well today and why they happened. Be specific. This is a proven positive psychology intervention.

challenges.title: Body scan meditation
challenges.description: Sit or lie down comfortably. Close your eyes and mentally scan from your toes to the top of your head, noticing sensations without judgment.

challenges.title: Identify your triggers
challenges.description: On paper, list three situations today where you reached for your phone or AI tool without thinking. What emotion preceded the action?

challenges.title: Define one deep work session
challenges.description: On paper, define one specific task you'll do next with zero digital distractions for at least 45 minutes. Write the start time and commit.

challenges.title: Gratitude walk
challenges.description: Walk slowly for 5 minutes while mentally listing everything you're grateful for in your immediate environment. Notice sounds, light, textures.
